input:
Mind Mapping

Challenge:

My challenge is linked to the concept of “what is the nature tourism?” I would like to develop  an innovative and added value tourism project linked to nature but, I do not have any specific  background or job experience linked to tourism. So, I have been searching and reading a lot of  information about what is nature tourism, trying to read online forums about this subject to  get some inputs from real nature tourists.

Selection:

To address main challenge I selected the Mind Mapping tool. This tool allows me to map all the  topics linked to the subject and to have a more clear view on their interactions and  connections. In this specific case, as I do not have any experience in this business, I need to  map all the information and data collected this tool helped me to organize the information and  the data gathered from this business and from online forums from nature tourists. Using it  permits me not only to have the information but to have a visual form of all the topics and  how they connect to each other. This tool is used during the “what is?” phase of design  thinking, so I realized that this should be the better tool for me to use while I want to better  understand what is nature tourism.

Application:

As I was reading the information and collecting the data, I was writing, what for me were the  most important topics, in post-it. After every reading I grouped the post-it by specific topics  and gave a name to each group of post-its. In the meantime, in a large piece of paper I wrote  the subject I wanted to better understand, “nature tourism”, at the center of this paper. Then I  fixed all the post-it in the paper, building up the Mind Map.

Insight:

From not knowing nothing about the nature tourism to have a good view of what are the main  topics that it is necessary to take care before entering this type of business. I got three main  insights while building and analyzing the Mind Map: different type of customers and their  requests, especially in terms of services; location is a key topic to enter and develop this type  of business and sustainability is a very important and trendy topic that needs to be addressed  in a very careful way. Having all these topics in a visual form gave me a more detailed vision on  what this business is about in a faster way.

Approach:

This time I did this exercise by myself and only based on the read information and data  gathered. I did it like this because I wanted to better investigate, starting from zero  information, about this type of business. Next time I will do it, trying to involve a group of  friends that usually do some nature tourism trips.

My name: Adelino Carneiro

input:
Aylin Osorio

Reflection-Learning Launch

Challenge

I´m a college student in my first year of International Business and Marketing  degree my challenge is to have a small or medium business where me and my mom  are partners. My mom is an entrepreneur, consultant and coach, she already has her  own business about computer science. We want to make customized grocery bags  painted by hand and establish a partnership with diverse drugstores and  supermarkets in which to sell our products and thus establish a contract in which  both entities benefit.

Selection

We have selected the learning launch tool, since it’s like a trial and error. A  hypothesis you can try in a quick way.

Application

For our principal learning launch, we focused on what people want. We have done  research and a lot of surveys. We started selling them as an alternative of plain  colored bags with the promise that they are unique, there will never be one like the  other; since there was recently a law about banning and fining the use of plastic  bags to avoid and minimize pollution and contamination in our country. Actually,  we´re having a pretty positive reaction from the public.

For our other learning launch, we are establishing contact with small family stores  and pharmacies and if they sell well, we are thinking of expanding to supermarkets  and pharmaceutical chains throughout the city. To do that, we participated in some  workshops for entrepreneurs offered by some organizations, in which we had the  opportunity to establish a network, in that way we were able to offer alliances and  carry out surveys with other businessmen.

Insight

Aylin Osorio

We realized that with this tool we could expand our limits. With this method, we  quickly knew what the results of our hypotheses were so that we could improve the  product every time something did not turn out well or did not have as great an  impact as we wanted. We learned which materials to start using, which processes  were the best in terms of quality/price and so on. We had ups and downs that we  could correct and improve with the fast results that the learning launch gave us.

Approach

This course gave me a new approach to design thinking and the learning launch  method. I think that, with this type of business, this is the right method to use. My  mom and me are having good sales for such a young business, we still have a lot to  improve but with this tool, I know we will go far.
target:

input: Name: Ravinder Pal      Challenge: Fixeen Project    The Fixeen project seeks to strengthen the labor system by injecting transparency into  it. To explain the above, we ask the following question: Is the labor system okay today?  the imminent answer is NO. Since, as, even we have witnessed ourselves, if you do not  have a positioned brand, an acquaintance who recommends you or market strategies  to which few have access, you can quite possibly be the best at what you do, but you  will hardly be able to survive.    So what solution does Fixeen propose to this problem? We created a web portal where  both empiricists, professionals and companies such as contractors and obviously  contractors, who would be the contracting users, can socialize.  What is the plus of this? We propose to generate a national ranking by work,  employment, or dedication where it is the same market that qualifies "the best in each  profession or occupation"      I selected the visualization tool    I chose it because I believe it is the best tool for exploring and expanding our ability to  innovate.    It is the easiest way to obtain information about something, it also allows us to think in  a non-verbal way and helps us access parts of our brain that we do not normally use  and this is very important in design.    The first thing I did was see the serious problem that the labor system has in most  countries and that at first glance it is clear that this system needs to be changed, I used  the visualization tool to realize the errors and then create a kind mind map and put a  logical and innovative solution to the problem.    Perspective: What caught my attention the most is that we usually get a lot of  information from many sources but they are not clear and putting a solution to it only  using a mind map or diagram and then putting it on paper seemed very fabulous.    What awaits me afterwards is to continue applying and creating ever more effective  solutions to everyday problems without having to resort to other forms of search that  are not interesting.

input:
DESIGN THINKING LAB EXPERIMENT – 4

STORYTELLER – Ajay Vishwkarma – 779875708882 – CS4

PERSON A – Zahida – 800306846075 – CS4

PERSON B – Zahida – 955487471144 – CS4

STORY TELLING – Zahida (9023)

We see a lot of people having mental health issues but do not tend to take  treatment from professionals. This is because of in-office treatment stigma,  lack of awareness and availability. But it is important to give equal importance  to both physical and mental health which is lacking in the society.

So, I came up with an idea of mental health app with professionals like  psychologists, psychotherapists, counsellors. Along with professionals we also  include caregivers because they play an important role in the treatment  process. The app also includes personality tests, mood trackers, positive  quotes and many more features. Through the app we can reach out to large  group of people with 24/7 availability at their comfort level.

USER  QUOTES  MEANING  CONCLUSION

Zahida (9043)  “Why would you  recommend a  mental health  application and how  would it be useful?”

“Why would you  recommend a  mental health  application and how  would it be useful?”

It helps the users to  connect with mental  health professionals  through an online  platform and engage  in therapy sessions  and activities like  personality tests,  mood trackers, and  other additional  features. Through  the app we can  reach out to large  group of people.

“Can these mental  health apps be seen  as an alternative to  therapy sessions?”

Are these  applications useful  in providing the  same cure and  nursing which are  usually extended by  the physical therapy  sessions?

These play an  important role by  providing innovative  solutions for the self- management of  mental health  disorders and  interactive therapy  sessions from  renowned doctors  and counsellors.

Zahida (9027)  “What are the  additional features  customers can  benefit from?

What are the  additional features  of the application  which can assist or  educate the users?

By including  activities to boost  the spirit of the  users like mood  trackers, personality  tests, in-app  rewards to increase  motivation and  providing help to  the caregivers  through this  platform.

“How is the revenue  and returns  run/controlled?”

what is the intricate  mechanism which  controls the revenue  and returns likely to  generated through  the application?

The mental health  app can be designed  as a subscription- based model where  the users can be  provided with hands  on experience and  then can be  reasonably charged  for few  consultations and  diagnosis from  professionals.

MORAL OF THE STORY:

It is important to give mental health equal importance with physical health and  make the treatment available to everyone.
target:

input:
EXAMPLE REFLECTION – FIXED MINDSET      Challenge:  I am Narayan Majid, I am going to tell you about fixed mindset. Before  completing the course, I had a completely fixed mindset. Now I have  changed my mindset because there is more unwanted challenge in fixed  mindset which I came to know from this course. There are those types of  challenge in fixed mindset. If you guys have fixed mindset then once think  about it.    Now I am challenging myself to change my mindset from fixed to growth  mindset.    Selection:  So, I select my mindset to be a growth mindset and knowledgeable  mindset. I select growth mind set because our professor taught about fixed  mindset and growth mindset and I am impressed by growth mindset and I  select growth mind set.    According to me, growth mindset is like hungry mind which is always  ready to gain knowledge and stay quite like it knows nothing. Everyone  wants success and best life. To achieve success, you have to do something  special today and that’s why I select growth mindset.    Application:  The general application of growth mindset is to increase knowledge,  experience, and always ready to gain knowledge. To gain this and make  something new on you, you have to challenge yourself to be great and  achieve your goal. I have not applied this tool yet. Currently I am studying  whenever I will start thinking about my business and innovation this idea  will be applied for it.    Insight and Approach:  I don’t have experience about business yes, I will make a group and start  my own later. For this I have to gain knowledge and experience about that  first. By completing this course now i can challenge myself at different  situation and all and i will approach my destination.    I will apply all my knowledge and experience which I gain from studying,  seniors, documentary, others experience, market visit and all so that I can

make something different. I will apply some different and modern tools to  develop and help others. Our society is not well developed on every  position and situation, to develop properly in every place we have to use  different ideas and proper knowledge.    In the end, take every risk as opportunity and challenge yourself to do  something new and to learn something new.

input:
Reflection – Learning Launch

Francisco Ferreira

Challenge

I take part of a social enterprenuership group in my university. We were in contact

with Capão das Antas, a rural Community in the suburbs of São Carlos (SP-Brazil). We had the  intention to criate, with the local producers, a project that wold improve the quality of life in  the neighborhood. For that, in the first two months we begin to listen each farmer of Capão  and try identify the principal issues of the place and their residentes. After some visits we  concluded that the principal problem was selling the products that they plant in the local  farms. Therefore, we had the objective to make and test a business model that would increase  their rent.

Selection

In order to build a sustainable business model with the farmers, we made a first test

were we delivered the products at the costumer’s door. The action had a good result but we  want to increase even more ours sales. So we used Learning Launchs to test new ideais and  then improve our business model.

Application

After we analise all the data we had and build the business model, Its was far from be

the most sustainable and profitable one. To improve our project, we made a plan to test new  ways of seling. So every week, after we analise all the process from the farm to the costumer  door, we propose and teste the improvement in the current business model.

In order to give examples of how it works, after some weeks we notice that the

farmers were having problems in separate the orders. The costumers were ordering with na  app called whatsapp, but the person who was responsable for taking note of the orders was  messy with the conversations feed. As soon as we notice this problem we proposed a new app  that automatically cluster the orders in a simple feed. Therefore we tested this improvement  in the next week and got zero mistakes in separating the orders.

Insights

The application of Learning Launches showed me how testing is importante in building

a solid business model. Therefore, I learned that the more you test possibles ideias, more  chance of sucess you have. Testing a lot of improvement and ideas is not the fastest way to  achieve a better scenario but is cleary a certainly strategy to get there.

Approach

During all the improvement plan that we act every week we propose changes based on

what we were seeing during the deliveries. I think that we could have achieved better results  by analysis more sources of data, like feedback from all the stakeholders. Besides that we  could have made a better knowledge management of the changes proposed for the business  model.
target:

I take part of a social enterprenuership group in my university. We were in contact

with Capão das Antas, a rural Community in the suburbs of São Carlos (SP-Brazil). We had the  intention to criate, with the local producers, a project that wold improve the quality of life in  the neighborhood. For that, in the first two months we begin to listen each farmer of Capão  and try identify the principal issues of the place and their residentes. After some visits we  concluded that the principal problem was selling the products that they plant in the local  farms. Therefore, we had the objective to make and test a business model that would increase  their rent.

Selection

In order to build a sustainable business model with the farmers, we made a first test

were we delivered the products at the costumer’s door. The action had a good result but we  want to increase even more ours sales. So we used Learning Launchs to test new ideais and  then improve our business model.

Application

After we analise all the data we had and build the business model, Its was far from be

the most sustainable and profitable one. To improve our project, we made a plan to test new  ways of seling. So every week, after we analise all the process from the farm to the costumer  door, we propose and teste the improvement in the current business model.

In order to give examples of how it works, after some weeks we notice that the

farmers were having problems in separate the orders. The costumers were ordering with na  app called whatsapp, but the person who was responsable for taking note of the orders was  messy with the conversations feed. As soon as we notice this problem we proposed a new app  that automatically cluster the orders in a simple feed. Therefore we tested this improvement  in the next week and got zero mistakes in separating the orders.

Insights

The application of Learning Launches showed me how testing is importante in building

a solid business model. Therefore, I learned that the more you test possibles ideias, more  chance of sucess you have. Testing a lot of improvement and ideas is not the fastest way to  achieve a better scenario but is cleary a certainly strategy to get there.

Approach

During all the improvement plan that we act every week we propose changes based on

what we were seeing during the deliveries. I think that we could have achieved better results  by analysis more sources of data, like feedback from all the stakeholders. Besides that we  could have made a better knowledge management of the changes proposed for the business  model.

input:
Date of submission: 30 October 2021

Author: Mdjakir Puerta

Design Thinking for Innovation – Coursera

Assignment

Mind Mapping

Challenge: In the Organization that I am working in, which is a highly regulated Pharma Industry, we  have several procedures and standards that need to be applied while implementing or developing a  computerized system. It has been a challenge for associates to know all the standards, regulations and  procedures, visualize them all at one place and apply them appropriately based on different scenarios.  Using Design Thinking and Mind Mapping tool technique tried to resolve this challenge by providing a  mind map of regulations, standards and procedures at one place in a single frame or window for  associates to refer.

Selection: Found Mind mapping tool to be very effective to solve this problem or challenges since the  mind mapping technique creates a very good analytical representation in a very user friendly way. Using  this tool, could successfully create a mind map of all regulations, standards and procedures including  different categories of computerized systems so that application development teams can apply them  easily rather than spending too much time figuring out what are the relevant standards, procedures, were  are they? What to apply in which scenario.

Application: Using the mind mapping tool, came up with a high-level pyramid structure and mapped all  the Standards, Procedures, Guidelines, Controls with different types of computerized systems and what is  relevant for different categories of computerized systems. Used Mindjet Manager Application for mapping.

Insight: The Design for Innovation course helped me to understand how to solve business  problems, challenges in day-to-day life using different tools and techniques, apply critical thinking  and design thinking principles. The Mind Mapping tool helped me to solve this problem, which was  saving lots of time for Application Development teams to see all relevant standards, procedures,  controls at one place and apply them to be compliant.

Approach: I would try to automate this entire process of applying relevant standards, procedures  based on the category of the computerized system being implemented by providing a mind map,  dashboard.

input:
Sarai Diaz

1. Challenge

We received a Housing Project in which the local citizens of a community were opposed  because they think it will affect the environment thanks to the corrupted information some  people has spread. The challenge the company presented us was to change the perception  of the Project in that community to have their support when the construction finally started.

2. Selection

I selected visualization because in order to change their perception first I need to  understand the real needs and concerns of the people and identify the messages they have  received from the opposing groups that are currently affecting the image of the Project.

3. Application

First, I needed to enlist all the stakeholders involved because it was not only the immediate  community but while digging I found out there also there were political interest, local  government, schools, and all kind of organizations that needed to be included in the  campaign. Then I needed to know what the people thought about the project in a deeply  perspective, because I couldn’t change what I didn’t know they think. That took me to small  interviews with key actor of the community and the identified groups to better understand  what we were dealing with, to let them express themselves, in these interviews we used  pictures of the project that lead us to emotions, when we showed images of the project  people were: angry, frustrated, sad and some of them tired of hearing about the project.

4. Insight

After that process I realized that the real mission was not to change their perception of the  Project but it was to really inform them of what was going to happen without the alarm,  misconceptions and mistakes the other group had already told them, people were angry,  sad, frustrated about the project. Also, being able to translate “the bad image of the  project” into something more specific like emotions, let me realize we needed an innovative  approach to capture their attention to be able to provide them the information.

5. Approach

Visualization is key before starting any activity to really understand what you are dealing  with and finding the best strategies to tackle it, but the problem is it takes time and your  boss, your client, or the one paying you will ask for immediate actions and immediate  results. So one of the most important things I would do next when I need to use visualization  is first sensibilize of the importance of visualization as a tool, not to guarantee success, but  to understand the challenge, your audience and guide you to a better solution.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Sarai Diaz⟨/NAME⟩

input:
Maribel Rodriguez   Reflection Paper

I am currently taking my Thesis and I am having a hard time choosing a topic or a problem. In

this case, I decided to use the mind mapping tool. I started by brainstorming topics that I am interested  in. Then, I particularized by creating sub-topics. By doing so, it allowed me to organize my ideas and  concepts visually.

Mind mapping helps me recall and study my ideas easier. It lessens the complications of my

design thinking process. It shows the topics I am most and least interested in. It gives my thoughts a  structure and helps me generate new ideas.

Also, when I am conflicted between two concepts, mind mapping helps me analyze and

compare all related factors so I can make the right decision.
target:
⟨NAME⟩Maribel Rodriguez⟨/NAME⟩   Reflection Paper

input:
Alejandra Padilla

Final Project: The family trip

Challenge and selection

The tool that I will use for this project is the mind mapping because I consider

that it is one of the most important alternatives, since it allows us to look for

patterns and perspectives to problems solution, and we can explore our creativity

with this. I like the mind maps because I use them for my schoolwork and is very

easy to use them. I think is the best way to synthesize information clearly. The

mind map it is a creative, intuitive, visual, and attractive way of presenting

information that is confusing.

The challenge that I chose to apply this tool is about choosing a destination to go

on a trip with the family, we want to be 7 days in the city, we are looking for a

city that has many things to do and know, like museums, restaurants, parks,

monuments, and others. We do not have a specific place, we just want to know

some place in Europe, and we can travel any season of the year.

Application

We must first clarify our ideas. The first step is to choose 5 alternatives of

countries or cities to which we want to go, then select 2 options that interest us

the most. The next step is making a mind map of each option. In each map we

will include basic things, starting with the places we can visit in that city, what

activities are there to do like: museums, restaurants, parks, and others; what is

the best season to travel, prices of flights, hotels. We are going to complement

the map with the information we find, trying to be brief. Once the maps are

ready, we will make a comparison with the information and choose the option

that best suits us.

List of 5 cities:

1. Madrid, Spain.

2. Paris, France.

3. Rome, Italy

Alejandra Padilla

4. Amsterdam, Netherlands

5. London, England

The two cities chosen:

1. Amsterdam, Netherlands

2. Rome, Italy

Mind map example:

Insight and Approach

I consider that the creation of mental maps greatly facilitates the process of

ordering ideas, because it allows to do it in a simple and creative way. It helps

to summarize the information clearly, which allows us to better analyze the

alternatives we have and help us make better decisions. In our case, organizing

the information in such a simple way allowed me to share it with the rest of my

family and that they could choose the destination they want to go to as a family.

Alejandra Padilla

Also, each person can contribute something that enriches the information; and

the elaboration can be individual or group.

In conclusion, it is a very useful and simple tool to make decisions, and without

a doubt I will continue to use it to synthesize information and clarify ideas, since

it allows to have a very enriching creative process. And probably, next time I will

try to apply this tool to some problem at work, and I will elaborate the mental

map in a group way.
target:

input:
Coursera

Design Thinking

Cherry Dantas

Assignment

Challenge:

As first of all I want to write that my personality is very sensitive. People and  friends around me call me that I am very silent lady. Some of them also called

me that I am unconfident. I need a phycatrist because I can’t express my

feelings easily to others. One of the main challenge of my life is that I want to  show people around me that I Confident however I am silent but I am creative. I

am intelligent. However I am not too much friendly but I am very deep.

So I started photography because I like photography and through photography I  express my feelings and my depth to others. Through my photography asthetic

skills I think that it becomes easier for me to express my thoughts through

visualization. As we all know that human mind easily grab the visual….. so now  by seeing my posts and photography with deep captions now everyone says to

me that you are creative, intelligent, my thinkings are deep.

Selection:

I selected visualization tool for my challenge description because this tool is

very effective to express our inner feelings and thought. Also through

visualization tool it is easy for the audience to understand what actually we

want to say and express. Also visuals likle images, videos, flowcharts, etc easily

grab the attention of the target audience.

Application:

I applied visualization tool and visualization technique to express my feelings,  my inner thoughts through photography. By taking photos of landscapes, nature,

sky, clouds, flowers and sometime random clicks. All these activities works  effectively. That I personally feel myself confident. And also the mind set of  people around me about myself was also changed.It becomes easier for the

people and for my friends to analayize my personality through my art works and

photography.

Insight:

I think that by using visualization tool it helps me to express my inner qualities  and feelings easily. The insight I gained is that visualization tool and process is

the most effective way to express your ideas, feelings, thoughts etc.

Approach:

So I think that by using the same tool I improve my work that how to express

my thoughts more effectively. Also by creating artworks, paintings, sketch

drawings and more visual elements I will express my ideas and thoughts more

and more effectively.

Thanks
